title: fabric-archive SKILL.md — E5 周期触发 appendix (/loop + OS cron samples)
description: Append a new H2 section at the end of SKILL.md documenting E5 (周期触发) usage. This is zero-code — fab CLI doesn't ship a cron helper. Instead, the SKILL.md gives explicit /loop sample (Claude Code native, primary path) and OS cron sample (cross-platform alternative). User chooses based on their environment. The section also defines '今日复盘' as a magic invocation phrase that Phase -0.5 recognizes as 'time_window = today'.

files.target: end of file, after existing Worked Examples section
files.change:
Add new H2 section '## E5 周期触发 (Scheduled Daily Recap)' with subsections:
  (1) Overview: '今日复盘 = E5 entry point. Default scope = today. Falls back to historical scan if today yields no candidates (silent-skip per Phase 2.5).'
  (2) /loop sample (recommended for Claude Code):
    ```
    /loop /fabric-archive 今日复盘 --cron "0 23 * * *"
    ```
  (3) OS cron sample (macOS launchd / Linux crontab):
    ```
    # crontab -e
    0 23 * * * cd /path/to/project && claude code -p "/fabric-archive 今日复盘" 2>&1 >> /var/log/fabric-daily-recap.log
    ```
  (4) E5 prompt parse contract: when user/cron invokes with '今日复盘' / 'daily recap' literal:
    - Phase -0.5 sets time_window = today (00:00 local timezone to current ts)
    - Phase 0.4 skipped (entry_point=E5)
    - Phase 2.5 always writes session_archive_attempted event (silent-skip path if no candidates)
  (5) Trade-off table (/loop vs OS cron):
    | 维度 | /loop | OS cron |
    |---|---|---|
    | 鉴权 | 复用 Claude session | 独立 token / login |
    | 跨平台 | Claude Code 全平台一致 | macOS launchd / Linux cron 不同 |
    | Token 成本 | 累积 (长 session) | 每 tick fresh, 无累积 |
    | 调试 | Claude UI 可见 | 日志文件 |
  (6) Failure modes documentation: '/loop session crash → 归档暂停, 用户需重启 /loop; OS cron 自带恢复, 但需独立 claude code CLI 安装与鉴权'.

implementation: Read current SKILL.md end-of-file to confirm appendix insertion point.
Write the H2 section with all 6 subsections per files[].change directive.
Verify the /loop sample syntax matches Claude Code /loop spec (cron flag string).
Verify the OS cron sample uses portable shell syntax (no bash-isms).
Add a 'NOT in scope' bullet: 'fab CLI does NOT provide a cron helper command — keeps fab focused on archive/install, not scheduling infrastructure.'

reference.pattern: Existing 'Worked Examples' H2 section at end of SKILL.md — same level/style/structure
reference.examples: rc.23 F8c S5 onboard documentation pattern — table + code blocks + decision matrix

rationale.chosen_approach: Zero fab code change — documentation only. User opts into E5 via their existing OS scheduler. /loop primary path leverages Claude Code's native cron-like feature; OS cron is fallback for non-Claude-Code environments.
alternatives_considered: fab CLI 'fab schedule daily 23:00' subcommand (rejected: violates cli-design 'don't make a scheduler', cross-platform OS integration is heavy)
Server daemon for scheduling (rejected: fab is a CLI tool, daemon is out of scope)
Skip E5 documentation (rejected: user explicitly requested E5 support in Q3 design)
decision_factors: fab端零代码 means rc.25 ships without new commands or test surface for scheduling
/loop 是 Claude Code 原生功能, samples are stable across Claude versions
User自主选 /loop vs cron based on environment
rationale.tradeoffs: Users must understand OS cron syntax or /loop semantics to use E5; mitigation: 2 ready-to-copy samples in SKILL.md.

manual_checks: Read appendix in actual Claude Code UI to verify markdown renders
Manually copy /loop sample into Claude Code and verify it accepts the cron flag
success_metrics: SKILL.md has new H2 '## E5 周期触发'
Both /loop and OS cron samples present

criteria: SKILL.md contains exact string '## E5 周期触发'

SKILL.md contains '今日复盘' as a recognized magic phrase
